title: Bridge Essay: Electrophysiological correlates of zen meditation: An investigation using in-mon

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Formless/Absorption** (Phase 8) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Formless/Absorption to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Electrophysiological correlates of zen meditation: An investigation using in-monastery EEG acquisition.** — Biol Psychol. PMID: 41101685. DOI: 10.1016/j.biopsycho.2025.109133. This study investigated the bioelectric brain activity associated with Zen meditation in an ecologically valid monastic setting, comparing experienced Zen practitioners with non-meditating controls. The experimental group consisted of Vietnamese monks from Truc Lam Tay Thien and Truc Lam Thuong Chieu monasteries, while the control group comprised non-practitioners. EEG data were collected using aportable 4-channel BrainBit Flex cap during both morning and evening meditation sessions, capturing
